acceptance: No code comment or doc-comment cites the retired layout (specs/architecture/NN-*, the bare n//l/ abbreviations, l.md, specs/intent/00-intent.md, or 'law N' numbering); every cite resolves to a current specs/** section. cargo build/test and emit --frozen are byte-unchanged (comments don't reach output).
notes: Authorized once by John (07-07 inbox) as an exception to the ride-only rule — a corpus-wide invalidation. Mapping: 10-contracts→contract.md, 15-kinds→representation.md, 20-surface+40-composition→pipeline.md, 45-governance→contract.md, 50-distribution→distribution.md, 00-intent+'law N'→intent.md invariants. Fix the pointer, keep the claim; leave fixture payload prose. Serialized behind LOCK-NESTING-TEMPLATES.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest — nothing moved this window.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.4); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs still pins SDK `^0.0.2` (0.0.4 published) — a release-owned bump, not this sweep.
